methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Skeleton Technologies is a Series E climate and energy company that has raised $200M+ to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector contains 21 peer companies across public and private markets, though the immediate comparable set is constrained: one public reference point (LNZA) and eight private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, a narrow peer-median approach is not applicable here—no same-stage comparables exist, only two adjacent-stage companies qualify, and none meet valuation-tier similarity thresholds, falling short of the three-company minimum for a reliable median benchmark.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total raised ($200M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.4x multiplier for Series E progression yields an implied valuation of $280M. This single-method triangulation is inherently constrained by limited comparables, introducing meaningful dependency on the assumed multiplier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $238M to $322M, centered on $280M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.4x stage step-up multiplier in a sector where frontier-tech companies often exhibit non-standard growth patterns, and the absence of direct peer validation. Any valuation within this band should be treated as indicative pending more robust comparable data or detailed financial performance metrics.
